provisions.title: ADDITION THE PENSION LIST
provisions.content:
-475-

J u d g e s of each Court of Appeal shall be three instesd of five.
        ARTICLE 2. Article 31 of the Law of Judicial Organiza-
 tion and Cassation Procedure of June 2, 1908, is hereby amended
 to read as follows:
        “Article 31. The Courts of Appeal shall be three and shall
 sit, one in Santo Domingo, one in Santiago and the third in Con-
 cepci6n de La Vega. Each shall consist of a President and two
 Judges, and a Procurador General. The first shall have juris-
 diction in the judicial districts of Santo Domingo, Seybo, Azua,
 Barahona and San Pedro de Macoris ; the second in those of San-
 tiago, Puerto Plata, Monte Cristy and Moca, and the third in
 tho;.e of La Vega, Pacificador and Samana.
        “Paragraph 1. Each Court of appeal shall have a Secret-
 ary, two clerks, two constables, and such other employees as may
 be necessary.
        “Paragraph 2. No Court of appeal can sit with less than
 three members, without counting the Procurador General.”
     ARTICLE 3. All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed.

                                    S. S. ROBISON,
                           Rear-Admiral, United States Navy,
                          Military Governor of Santo Doming2      .
‘Santo Domingo, D. R.,
.October 27, 1921.

     MILITARY GOVERNMENT O F SANTO DOMINGO.
                Executive Order No. 684.
           ADDITION TO T H E PENSIOR LIST.
                    0. G . No. 3271.

    By virtue of tLe powers vested in t h e Military Government
of Santo Domingo, the following Executive Order is hereby pro-
mulgated.
    ARTICLE 1. It is hereby provided that the person ment-
ioned below shall have the right to receive quarterly from the
National Treasury, the approvgl and rewmmenldation of the
Secretary of State of Interior and Police having first been ob-

                                 -4'76-

tained, t h e sum t h a t appears beside her name, which s u m shalt
be charged to the pensions Article of the Budget as a pension in
accordance with the provisions of Executive Order No. 456 ;
     Pension No. 800, Sefiora Orfelina R. Vda. F o r t h , $30.00 I
month and $360.00 a year.
     It is understood t h a t in the case of this person, the said pen-
sion shall be ef1ective beg;iining Octobzr 14, 1921.

                                          S. S. ROBISON,
                              Rear-Admiral, United States Navy,
                             Military Governor of Santo Domingo,

S a n t o Doiningo, D. R.,
November 3, 1921.
